backgroundsublingual allergen immunotherapy is an effective treatment against allergic respiratory disease . many studies have shown the safety of this type of therapy , although the factors that might affect the tolerability of high - dose sublingual immunotherapy have not been well established . the aim of this study was to determine the factors that affect the tolerability of sublingual allergen immunotherapy.patients and methodsa total of 183 subjects aged 5 years , diagnosed with allergic rhinitis with / without mild to moderate asthma due to sensitization to grass , olive pollen , or mites , were included in this open , retrospective , multicentric , noninterventional study . sublingual immunotherapy was administered for at least 3 months.resultsthe most frequent adverse reaction was oral pruritus ( 13.7% of the patients ) . most of the reactions were local ( 84.7% ) and immediate ( 93.5% ) and occurred during the initiation phase ( 60.6% ) . all reactions were mild to moderate in severity . no serious adverse reactions were registered . when comparing factors with potential influence on the occurrence of adverse reactions , the results between the groups of subjects with and without adverse reactions showed no statistically significant differences in sex ( p=0.6417 ) , age ( p=0.1801 ) , years since the disease was first diagnosed ( p=0.3800 ) , treatment composition ( p=0.6946 ) , polysensitization ( p=0.1730 ) , or clinical diagnosis ( p=0.3354 ) . however , it was found that treatment duration had a statistically significant influence ( 3 months , > 3 months : p=0.0442 ) and the presence of asthma was close to statistical significance ( p=0.0847).conclusionin our study , treatment duration is significantly associated with the occurrence of adverse reactions after the administration of high doses of sublingual allergen immunotherapy .

Background: Frontotemporal dementia (FTD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by cognitive, behavioral, and language impairments. Mutations in the MAPT gene, which encodes tau protein, have been associated with FTD. Tau protein is found in neurons and other cell types throughout the central nervous system. In disease, tau protein forms aggregates in the brain, leading to neurodegeneration and neuronal loss. Aggregates of tau are a hallmark pathology in a range of neurodegenerative diseases, including Alzheimer’s disease and chronic traumatic encephalopathy.

sap flow measurements have become integral in many physiological and ecological investigations . a number of methods are used to estimate sap flow rates in trees , but probably the most popular is the thermal dissipation ( td ) method because of its affordability , relatively low power consumption , and ease of use . however , there have been questions about the use of this method in ring - porous species and whether individual species and site calibrations are needed . we made concurrent measurements of sap flow rates using td sensors and the tissue heat balance ( thb ) method in two oak species ( quercus prinus willd . and quercus velutina lam . ) and one pine ( pinus echinata mill . ) . we also made concurrent measurements of sap flow rates using both 1 and 2-cm long td sensors in both oak species . we found that both the td and thb systems tended to match well in the pine individual , but sap flow rates were underestimated by 2-cm long td sensors in five individuals of the two ring - porous oak species . underestimations of 2035% occurred in q. prinus even when a clearwater correction was applied to account for the shallowness of the sapwood depth relative to the sensor length and flow rates were underestimated by up to 50% in q. velutina . two centimeter long td sensors also underestimated flow rates compared with 1-cm long sensors in q. prinus , but only at large flow rates . when 2-cm long sensor data in q. prinus were scaled using the regression with 1-cm long data , daily flow rates matched well with the rates measured by the thb system . daily plot level transpiration estimated using td sap flow rates and scaled 1 cm sensor data averaged about 15% lower than those estimated by the thb method . therefore , these results suggest that 1-cm long sensors are appropriate in species with shallow sapwood , however more corrections may be necessary in ring - porous species .

abstractobjectivesto determine prevalence of dental erosion among competitive swimmers in kaunas , the second largest city in lithuania.material and methodsthe study was designed as a cross - sectional survey , with a questionnaire and clinical examination protocols . the participants were 12 - 25 year - old swimmers regularly practicing in the swimming pools of kaunas . of the total of 132 participants there were 76 ( 12 - 17 year - old ) and 56 ( 18 - 25 year - old ) individuals ; in groups 1 and 2 , respectively . participants were examined for dental erosion , using a portable dental unit equipped with fibre - optic light , compressed air and suction , and standard dental instruments for oral inspection . lussi index was applied for recording dental erosion . the completed questionnaires focused on the common erosion risk factors were returned by all participants.resultsdental erosion was found in 25% of the 12 - 17 year - olds , and in 50% of 18 - 25 years - olds . mean value of the surfaces with erosion was 6.31 ( sd 4.37 ) . all eroded surfaces were evaluated as grade 1 . swimming training duration and the participants age correlated positively ( kendall correlation , r = 0.65 , p < 0.001 ) , meaning that older swimmers had practiced for longer period . no significant correlation between occurrence of dental erosion and the analyzed risk factors ( gastroesophageal reflux disease , frequent vomiting , dry mouth , regular intake of acidic medicines , carbonated drinks ) was found in both study groups.conclusionsprevalence of dental erosion of very low degree was high among the regular swimmers in kaunas , and was significantly related to swimmers age .

Recent studies have demonstrated the importance of gut microbiome composition in the pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Our study utilized shotgun metagenomic sequencing to investigate the differences in microbial community composition between healthy controls and patients with IBD. We recruited 20 participants with Crohn's disease, 20 with ulcerative colitis, and 20 healthy controls, and performed shotgun metagenomic sequencing of their fecal samples. We used the HUMAnN pipeline to analyze the resulting sequence reads and determined the abundance of taxa at the genus level. Our results showed a significantly reduced abundance of Prevotella in patients with Crohn's disease compared to healthy controls. We also found an increased abundance of Faecalibacterium in patients with ulcerative colitis compared to healthy controls. Our findings suggest that gut microbiome composition plays a crucial role in the pathogenesis of IBD, and further research is needed to identify specific microbes that could serve as therapeutic targets for this disease.

introductionmagnetic resonance angiography ( mra ) screening for recurrence of a coiled intracranial aneurysm and formation of new aneurysms long - term after coiling may induce anxiety and depression . in coiled patients , we evaluated effects on mood and level of anxiety from long - term follow - up mra in comparison to general population norms.methodsof 162 patients participating in a long - term ( > 4.5 years ) mra follow - up after coiling , 120 completed the eq-5d questionnaire , a visual analog health scale and a self - developed screening related questionnaire at the time of mra . three months later , the same questionnaires were completed by 100 of these 120 patients . results were compared to general population norms adjusted for gender and age.resultsany problem with anxiety or depression was reported in 56 of 120 patients ( 47% ; 95%ci3856% ) at baseline and 42 of 100 patients ( 42% ; 95%ci3252% ) at 3 months , equally for screen - positives and -negatives . compared to the reference population , participants scored 38% ( 95%ci967% ) and 27% ( 95%ci450% ) more often any problem with anxiety or depression . three months after screening , 21% ( 20 of 92 ) of screen - negatives and 13% ( one of eight ) of screen - positives reported to be less afraid of subarachnoid hemorrhage ( sah ) compared to before screening . one of eight screen - positives reported increased fear of sah.conclusionspatients with coiled intracranial aneurysms participating in long - term mra screening reported significantly more often to be anxious or depressed than a reference group . screening did not significantly increase anxiety or depression temporarily . however , subjectively , patients did report an increase in anxiety caused by screening , which decreased after 3 months .

recent studies have suggested that the kisspeptin ( kp ) and kissorphin ( kso ) peptides have neuroprotective actions against the alzheimer 's amyloid- ( a ) peptide . overexpression of the human kiss-1 gene that codes for kp and kso peptides in sh - sy5y neurons has also been shown to inhibit a neurotoxicity . the in vivo actions of kp include activation of neuroendocrine and neurotransmitter systems . the present study used antagonists of kp , neuropeptide ff ( npff ) , opioids , oxytocin , estrogen , adrenergic , cholinergic , dopaminergic , serotonergic , and -aminobutyric acid ( gaba ) receptors plus inhibitors of catalase , cyclooxygenase , nitric oxide synthase , and the mitogen activated protein kinase cascade to characterize the kiss-1 gene overexpression neuroprotection against a cell model . the results showed that kiss-1 overexpression is neuroprotective against a and the action appears to involve the kp or kso peptide products of kiss-1 processing . the mechanism of neuroprotection does not involve the activation of the kp or npff receptors . opioids play a role in the toxicity of a in the kiss-1 overexpression system and opioid antagonists naloxone or naltrexone inhibited a toxicity . the mechanism of kiss-1 overexpression induced protection against a appears to have an oxytocin plus a cyclooxygenase dependent component , with the oxytocin antagonist atosiban and the cyclooxygenase inhibitor sc-560 both enhancing the toxicity of a.

ectopic pregnancy is a potentially life - threatening condition . detection of ectopic pregnancy on ct images is rare . in this case , we describe the ct findings of interstitial pregnancy both before and after rupture . if ct images demonstrate the presence of a strong enhancing ring - like mass in the pelvis , ectopic pregnancy should be considered .

malaria and schistosomiasis are two of the most socioeconomically devastating parasitic diseases in tropical and subtropical countries . since current chemotherapeutic options are limited and defective , there is an urgent need to develop novel antiplasmodials and antischistosomals . hemozoin is a disposal product formed from the hemoglobin digestion by some blood - feeding parasites . hemozoin formation is an essential process for the parasites to detoxify free heme , which is a reliable therapeutic target for identifying novel antiparasitic agents . a series of novel carbazole aminoalcohols were designed and synthesized as potential antiplasmodial and antischistosomal agents , and several compounds showed potent in vitro activities against plasmodium falciparum 3d7 and dd2 strains and adult and juvenile schistosoma japonicum . investigations on the dual antiparasitic mechanisms showed the correlation between inhibitory activity of -hematin formation and antiparasitic activity . inhibiting hemozoin formation was identified as one of the mechanisms of action of carbazole aminoalcohols . compound 7 displayed potent antiplasmodial ( pf3d7 ic50 = 0.248 m , pfdd2 ic50 = 0.091 m ) and antischistosomal activities ( 100% mortality of adult and juvenile schistosomes at 5 and 10 g / ml , respectively ) and exhibited low cytotoxicity ( cc50 = 7.931 m ) , which could be considered as a promising lead for further investigation . stoichiometry determination and molecular docking studies were also performed to explain the mode of action of compound 7 .

gitelman 's syndrome is an autosomal recessive disorder characterized by hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis , hypokalemia , hypomagnesaemia , hypocalciuria , hyperreninemia and without hypertension . gitelman 's syndrome is caused by mutations of the slc12a3 gene , which encodes the na / cl co - transporter ( ncct ) in the distal convoluted tubule . majority of cases manifest during adolescence or adulthood and growth retardation is not the common feature . we report a rare presentation of gitelman 's syndrome in a four - year - old boy with growth retardation .

introduction : spinal subdural hematoma is a rare cause of radiculopathy and spinal cord compression syndromes . it s early diagnosis is essential . chronological appearance of these bleeds vary on mri.case report : a 56 year old man presented with progressive left lower limb radiculopathy and paraesthesias with claudication of three days duration . mri revealed a subdural space occupying lesion compressing the cauda equina at l5-s1 level producing a y shaped dural sac ( y sign ) , which was hyperintense on t1w imaging and hypointense to cord on t2w image . the stir sequence showed hyperintensity to cord . there was no history of bleeding diathesis . the patient underwent decompressive durotomy and biopsy which confirmed the diagnosis.conclusion:spinal subdural hematoma may present with rapidly progressive neurological symptoms . mri is the investigation of choice . the knowledge of mri appearance with respect to the chronological stage of the bleed is essential to avoid diagnostic and hence surgical dilemma

type 2 diabetes mellitus ( dm ) is a public health concern worldwide and an important cause of morbidity and mortality . type 2 dm is associated with microvascular and macrovascular complications . diabetic nephropathy ( dn ) , which is characterized by proteinuria , is one of the most serious long - term microvascular complications of dm . the proportion of dn is increasing worldwide . dn is the leading cause of chronic kidney diseases and end - stage renal disease , which constitutes the major workload of dialysis centers worldwide . microalbuminuria ( ma ) is the earliest sign of dn , so the early detection of ma and early control of diabetes retards the progression of dn .

Background: Frontotemporal dementia (FTD) is an irreversible and progressive neurodegenerative disease associated with aberrant accumulation of tau protein in the brain. Although familial FTD is associated with disease-linked mutations in the MAPT gene, the mechanisms that contribute to the accumulation of aberrant tau protein in sporadic FTD remain unknown.

r3d - blast is a blast - like search tool that allows the user to quickly and accurately search against the pdb for rna structures sharing similar substructures with a specified query rna structure . the basic idea behind r3d - blast is that all the rna 3d structures deposited in the pdb are first encoded as 1d structural sequences using a structural alphabet of 23 distinct nucleotide conformations , and blast is then applied to these 1d structural sequences to search for those rna substructures whose 1d structural sequences are similar to that of the query rna substructure . r3d - blast takes as input an rna 3d structure in the pdb format and outputs all substructures of the hits similar to that of the query with a graphical display to show their structural superposition . in addition , each rna substructure hit found by r3d - blast has an associated e - value to measure its statistical significance . r3d - blast is now available online at http://genome.cs.nthu.edu.tw/r3d-blast/ for public access .

Background: Primary prevention has been established to be an effective method for reducing cardiovascular disease (CVD) morbidity and mortality. In addition to traditional risk factors, studies have shown the importance of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol in predicting CVD risk. The use of statins, which lower LDL cholesterol, has been shown to be effective in preventing CVD in patients with elevated CVD risk. However, the use of statins in patients with low CVD risk has remained controversial.

Since its discovery, the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) has become a global pandemic and threat to human health. Correspondingly, researchers have made significant efforts to understand and develop effective strategies to fight the virus. While many vaccines have been developed and implemented, some people are still testing positive for SARS-CoV-2. Additionally, some individuals who initially tested negative are now testing positive for SARS-CoV-2. These individuals often experience prolonged symptoms that do not fit the typical COVID-19 description, and they are referred to as having “long COVID.”

Title: Editing Hemoglobin Pathway with CRISPR Technology for Treatment of Sickle Cell Disease: A Preclinical Study 1.Background: Sickle cell disease (SCD) is a serious genetic disease affecting millions worldwide. SCD is caused by a point mutation in the hemoglobin gene leading to the production of sickle-shaped red blood cells. 2.Methods: We have developed a novel CRISPR-based approach to correct the hemoglobin pathway in SCD patients. This approach uses CRISPR technology to edit the hemoglobin B (HBB) gene in hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs), the source of blood cells. Our approach is based on a two-step process. First, HSCs are genetically modified to produce a protein called β-thalassemia protein (Tα1). The Tα1 protein inactivates the abnormal sickle-shaped red blood cells. Second, the same HSCs are genetically modified to correct the HBB gene, restoring normal red blood cell production. 3.Results: We have successfully demonstrated our two-step approach

intra - arterial injections during ct scan examinations are a rare occurrence in day - to - day radiology practice but are of potential significance when they do occur . they render an imaging study non - diagnostic , and for imaging techniques like ct scan , expose patient to undue radiation dose . equally disturbing for both patient and clinicians , including radiologists , is the need for repeat imaging . for certain emergency indications , such an event may yield erroneous results and severely hamper patient 's management . we report one such event that occurred in our cardiac ct scan section with the aim to raise awareness about such events among our colleagues .

Title: Prevention of Type 2 Diabetes Complications with Metformin Adjunct Therapy: A Meta-Analysis. Background: Type 2 diabetes (T2D) is a rapidly growing global epidemic associated with serious complications. Background therapy for T2D typically includes lifestyle modification and medications that target glucose control. However, these treatments often do not achieve or maintain desired outcomes. Objective: To assess the efficacy of metformin as an adjunct therapy in preventing diabetes complications. Methods: Electronic databases including Cochrane, PubMed, and Embase were searched from inception to May 2020. Randomized controlled trials (RCTs) were included if they compared metformin and control therapies in patients with T2D. The primary outcome of interest was prevention of microvascular complications including diabetic nephropathy, neuropathy, and retinopathy. The secondary outcome of interest was prevention of macrovascular complications including myocardial infarction, stroke, and peripheral vascular disease. Results: A total of 14 RCTs were included in the meta-analysis. Metformin therapy was associated

Title: Urinary Cystatin C and Klotho Predict Renal Function Decline in Chronic Kidney Disease PatientsBackground: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a significant public health burden with high morbidity and mortality rates. Early prediction and intervention of CKD progression are crucial to improve patient outcomes. The search for reliable biomarkers of CKD progression continues, with urinary cystatin C and Klotho showing promise.Methods: This prospective cohort study included 271 CKD patients with albuminuria. Urinary cystatin C and Klotho were measured at baseline and 12, 24, and 36 months of follow-up. The decline in eGFR was the primary outcome.Results: The median age of patients was 68 years, and 55% were male. During follow-up, 18.5%, 33.6%, and 47.9% of patients developed an eGFR decline of >5, >10, and >15 mL/min/1.73 m2, respectively. In multivariable analysis,

post - traumatic stress disorder ( ptsd ) is a leading cause of sustained impairment , distress , and poor quality of life in military personnel , veterans , and civilians . indirect functional neuroimaging studies using pet or fmri with fear - related stimuli support a ptsd neurocircuitry model that includes amygdala , hippocampus , and ventromedial prefrontal cortex ( vmpfc ) . however , it is not clear if this model can fully account for ptsd abnormalities detected directly by electromagnetic - based source imaging techniques in resting - state . the present study examined resting - state magnetoencephalography ( meg ) signals in 25 active - duty service members and veterans with ptsd and 30 healthy volunteers . in contrast to the healthy volunteers , individuals with ptsd showed : 1 ) hyperactivity from amygdala , hippocampus , posterolateral orbitofrontal cortex ( ofc ) , dorsomedial prefrontal cortex ( dmpfc ) , and insular cortex in high - frequency ( i.e. , beta , gamma , and high - gamma ) bands ; 2 ) hypoactivity from vmpfc , frontal pole ( fp ) , and dorsolateral prefrontal cortex ( dlpfc ) in high - frequency bands ; 3 ) extensive hypoactivity from dlpfc , fp , anterior temporal lobes , precuneous cortex , and sensorimotor cortex in alpha and low - frequency bands ; and 4 ) in individuals with ptsd , meg activity in the left amygdala and posterolateral ofc correlated positively with ptsd symptom scores , whereas meg activity in vmpfc and precuneous correlated negatively with symptom score . the present study showed that meg source imaging technique revealed new abnormalities in the resting - state electromagnetic signals from the ptsd neurocircuitry . particularly , posterolateral ofc and precuneous may play important roles in the ptsd neurocircuitry model .

purposewe report a rare case of traumatic injury to the eye caused by homemade fireworks in a chinese juvenile patient with a metal ring left in the orbit after having been sutured at the emergency department.methodsan 11-year - old boy presented with a traumatic injury to the right eye from homemade fireworks . following initial assessment involving maxillofacial computed tomography ( ct ) and suturing at the emergency department , he was transferred to our department for further evaluation because of his poor sight 1 day later . on examination , a skin laceration beneath the right eyebrow was noted , but the superior orbit was not fully visible on the maxillofacial ct performed 1 day previously . therefore , an orbital ct scan was carried out on the second day , which showed a hyperdense ring embedded in the superior border of the orbital wall ; the ring was surgically removed . on postoperative day 7 , a fundus examination revealed resolving vitreous hemorrhage , blunt traumatic retinal detachment , and a large retinal tear superior to the macula . the patient refused to take surgery for retinal detachment into consideration . therefore , we opted for oral steroids and careful observation.resultsafter 2 months observation , the large retinal tear had healed and white fibrous scar tissue had developed , and the retinal detachment superior to the macula had reattached itself spontaneously . the patient 's vision had further improved to 20/200 . during 1 year of follow - up , he remained clinically stable.conclusionto avoid missing the diagnosis , a complete history of the mechanism of injury and accurate imaging still prove most useful . complete removal of the foreign body by the emergency physician is necessary because of the ocular toxicity of an iron - containing foreign body . as evidenced by the current case , oral steroids and observation for a period of several months is a management of choice for traumatic retinal detachment and retinal tear superior to the macula associated with homemade fireworks in children .

a set of protocols for catalytic enantioselective allylic substitution ( eas ) reactions that allow for additions of alkenyl units to readily accessible allylic electrophiles is disclosed . transformations afford 1,4-dienes that contain a tertiary carbon stereogenic site and are promoted by 1.05.0 mol % of a copper complex of an n - heterocyclic carbene ( nhc ) . aryl- as well as alkyl - substituted electrophiles bearing a di- or trisubstituted alkene may be employed . reactions can involve a variety of robust alkenyl(pinacolatoboron ) [ alkenyl b(pin ) ] compounds that can be either purchased or prepared by various efficient , site- , and/or stereoselective catalytic reactions , such as cross - metathesis or proto - boryl additions to terminal alkynes . vinyl- , e- , or z - disubstituted alkenyl- , 1,1-disubstituted alkenyl- , acyclic , or heterocyclic trisubstituted alkenyl groups may be added in up to > 98% yield , > 98:2 sn2:sn2 , and 99:1 enantiomeric ratio ( er ) . nhc cu - catalyzed eas with alkenyl b(pin ) reagents containing a conjugated carboxylic ester or aldehyde group proceed to provide the desired 1,4-diene products in good yield and with high enantioselectivity despite the presence of a sensitive stereogenic tertiary carbon center that could be considered prone to epimerization . in most instances , the alternative approach of utilizing an alkenylmetal reagent ( e.g. , an al - based species ) represents an incompatible option . the utility of the approach is illustrated through applications to enantioselective synthesis of natural products such as santolina alcohol , semburin , nyasol , heliespirone a , and heliannuol e.

the appearance of surface - induced phases of molecular crystals is a frequently observed phenomenon in organic electronics . however , despite their fundamental importance , the origin of such phases is not yet fully resolved . the organic molecule 6,6-dibromoindigo ( tyrian purple ) forms two polymorphs within thin films . at growth temperatures of 150 c , the well - known bulk structure forms , while at a substrate temperature of 50 c , a surface - induced phase is observed instead . in the present work , the crystal structure of the surface - induced polymorph is solved by a combined experimental and theoretical approach using grazing incidence x - ray diffraction and molecular dynamics simulations . a comparison of both phases reveals that stacking and hydrogen bonds are common motifs for the intermolecular packing . in - situ temperature studies reveal a phase transition from the surface - induced phase to the bulk phase at a temperature of 210 c ; the irreversibility of the transition indicates that the surface - induced phase is metastable . the crystallization behavior is investigated ex - situ starting from the sub - monolayer regime up to a nominal thickness of 9 nm using two different silicon oxide surfaces ; island formation is observed together with a slight variation of the crystal structure . this work shows that surface - induced phases not only appear for compounds with weak , isotropic van der waals bonds , but also for molecules exhibiting strong and highly directional hydrogen bonds .

Background: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is one of the leading causes of blindness worldwide. It is characterized by damage of the retinal microvasculature and neurodegeneration. Vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) has been implicated as a major mediator in the pathogenesis of DR.

The coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) is a global pandemic that has caused over 3.7 million deaths worldwide as of September 2021 (1). While most patients experience mild symptoms and recover from the infection, a significant subset of COVID-19 patients develop long-term symptoms following the acute phase of infection, known as long COVID (2). The exact pathophysiology of long COVID is unknown, but it is hypothesized to be due to ongoing tissue damage and systemic inflammation caused by the virus (3).

In this study, we developed a novel gene editing strategy to treat sickle cell disease (SCD) by correcting the hemoglobin (Hb) gene. Our method is based on the CRISPR-Cas9 technology, which allows for precise and specific DNA cleavage at target sites. We designed a single guide RNA (sgRNA) to target the site of the β-globin mutation that causes SCD. We then introduced the CRISPR-Cas9 system into human embryonic stem cells (hESCs) and induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) from SCD patients. We found that the CRISPR-Cas9 system was able to efficiently correct the Hb gene, leading to the production of normal hemoglobin (HbA). The corrected cells were then differentiated into red blood cells (RBCs), which displayed normal morphology and oxygen-carrying capacity. Furthermore, the CRISPR-Cas9 system was also able to correct the Hb gene in primary CD34+ hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells (HSPCs) from SCD patients. Overall

counterproductive work behavior ( cwb ) has long been recognized as a broad spectrum of job behaviors and its link with negative affectivity and hostile behaviors . it is a major concern practically for all organizations . repeated exposure to workplace stressor can result in a strain , an outcome of the job stress process that can be psychological , physical , or behavioral in nature , leading to cwbs . yoga is a technique that brings an improvement on mental and physical level by means of posture , breathing control methods , and silencing the mind through meditation . though yoga has received less scientific consideration , there has been a significant growth in the study of yoga in the healthy population . mindfulness and self - control practices like yoga encourage individuals to be aware and accept their aggression linked thoughts and emotions simply as a short - lived state rather than to control them . the positive effects of yoga on the improvement of personality traits are already proven . this paper introduces a simple model of cost - effective , trials of yoga intervention at the workplace which could result in the twin benefits of substantial savings from losses for the employers by reducing the cwb and health improvements for the employees by reducing the negative affectivity and aggression . internet databases such as pubmed , google scholar , and apa psycnet were accessed . the available data were systematically reviewed in a structured manner and analyzed .

we present three cases of thyrotoxic periodic paralysis ( tpp ) due to painless thyroiditis presenting as acute quadriparesis . all responded to potassium supplementation and propranolol . tpp may be due to thyrotoxicosis of any etiology , commonly grave 's disease . the absence of clinical signs of thyrotoxicosis can delay diagnosis and treatment . thyroid function tests should be a routine evaluation in all cases of hypokalemic periodic paralysis .

Tau protein is a microtubule-associated protein involved in regulating axonal transport and synaptic function. Hyperphosphorylation of tau protein leads to tau aggregation, which is a pathological hallmark of frontotemporal dementia (FTD) and other tauopathies. Understanding the molecular mechanisms of tau aggregation may aid in developing therapeutic strategies for FTD and other tauopathies. We investigated tau aggregation in the brains of FTD patients and control subjects. We also developed a novel cell model to study the molecular mechanisms of tau aggregation. Our results showed that hyperphosphorylation of tau protein was associated with tau aggregation in the brains of FTD patients. We also identified a novel role of microglia in tau aggregation, suggesting that microglia-dependent inflammation may contribute to tau aggregation. We also found that inhibiting tau phosphorylation decreased tau aggregation in our cell model. Our findings suggest that targeting tau phosphorylation and inflammation may be effective therapeutic strategies for FTD and other tauopathies.

Title: Programmable DNA Repair: A Novel CRISPR Therapy for Sickle Cell Disease (SCD) Patients.Objective: SCD is a hereditary blood disorder causing red blood cells (RBCs) to sickle under certain conditions. The misshapen RBCs reduce blood flow, causing acute pain, tissue damage, and premature organ failure. Gene-editing offers a promising therapy. However, most current methods cannot deliver therapeutic levels of gene-editing to a high percentage of RBCs without cytotoxicity. Our objective was to create a novel Cas9-based CRISPR therapy for SCD.Methods: We used a CRISPR-dCas9-transactivator fusion complex targeted to the promoter of HBG, the gene for fetal hemoglobin, to upregulate HBG expression and thereby produce fetal hemoglobin (HbF). HbF and adult hemoglobin (HbA) form more normal RBCs and do not sickle under the same conditions. We optimized the transactivator sequence and delivered CRISPR to primary human RBCs using c

with the development of biotechnologies and computational prediction algorithms , the number of experimental and computational prediction rna - associated interactions has grown rapidly in recent years . however , diverse rna - associated interactions are scattered over a wide variety of resources and organisms , whereas a fully comprehensive view of diverse rna - associated interactions is still not available for any species . hence , we have updated the raid database to version 2.0 ( raid v2.0 , www.rna-society.org/raid/ ) by integrating experimental and computational prediction interactions from manually reading literature and other database resources under one common framework . the new developments in raid v2.0 include ( i ) over 850-fold rna - associated interactions , an enhancement compared to the previous version ; ( ii ) numerous resources integrated with experimental or computational prediction evidence for each rna - associated interaction ; ( iii ) a reliability assessment for each rna - associated interaction based on an integrative confidence score ; and ( iv ) an increase of species coverage to 60 . consequently , raid v2.0 recruits more than 5.27 million rna - associated interactions , including more than 4 million rna rna interactions and more than 1.2 million rna protein interactions , referring to nearly 130 000 rna / protein symbols across 60 species .

Background: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a common complication of diabetes and the main cause of blindness in working-age adults. Chronic hyperglycemia leads to early retinal vascular damage, and patients with poorly controlled diabetes are at high risk of developing proliferative diabetic retinopathy (PDR). PDR is characterized by neovascularization, vitreous hemorrhage, and fibrotic scar tissue, leading to tractional retinal detachment and blindness.

background : nonoccupational anthracosis and silicosis has been reported from various parts of the world including ladakh in jammu and kashmir , india ; however , anthracosilicosis has only been reported in industrial workers till date.materials and methods : six cases from the ladakh region in jammu and kashmir , india with similar clinico - radiological - pathological features , i.e. , anthracosilicosis / anthracofibrosis have been analyzed . of these , four were analyzed retrospectively and two prospectively.result:all the patients were homemakers and resided in ladakh in jammu and kashmir , india since birth with an age range of 4262 years and an average age of 56 years . their average duration of symptoms was 4 years . spirometry showed small and/or large airway disease in 5/6 cases . on computed tomography ( ct ) , 4/6 cases showed progressive massive fibrosis ( pmf ) with calcified mediastinal lymph nodes . there were random or centrilobular nodules in all the six cases . bronchoscopy in 5/6 cases showed multiple anthracotic pigments with narrowing and distortion of the bronchus ( anthracofibrosis ) . malignancy was suspected clinico - radiologically in four cases and pathologically in two cases . on histopathology , anthracosis was demonstrated in all and silicosis in three cases.conclusion:anthracosilicosis can occur due to environmental exposure . ladakh in jammu and kashmir , india is the only place across the globe with unique environmental features having the presence of both free silica and biomass fuel . the disease was observed predominantly in older women . awareness would prevent unnecessary investigation for malignancy . treatment with the bronchodilator is useful as it has evidence of airway disease . finally , environmental measures and a proper study need to be undertaken for knowing the relative role of silica versus soot in causing the lung disease and preventing this irreversible condition .

Background: The development of a novel therapeutic approach that targets the epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) has become a key focus in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) research. The activation of EGFR, a cell surface receptor tyrosine kinase that binds to EGF and other growth factors, has been shown to be essential for tumor growth and progression. Previous studies have shown that NSCLC patients with EGFR mutations have increased sensitivity to EGFR-targeted therapies, including monoclonal antibodies and small molecule tyrosine kinase inhibitors. However, the efficacy of these therapies remains uncertain in NSCLC patients without EGFR mutations.

Title: The gut microbiome and inflammatory bowel disease: a case-control study from the human microbiome project. Background: We studied the relationship between gut microbiome composition and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in the human microbiome project. Methods: Participants with IBD and controls were recruited. Gut microbiome composition was studied using 16S rRNA sequencing. Results: There were significant differences in gut microbiome diversity between IBD cases and controls. IBD patients had a higher abundance of Akkermansia and a lower abundance of Bacteroides. IBD patients had a higher alpha diversity than controls, but no difference was found in beta diversity. IBD participants had a higher abundance of bacteria from the Clostridium genus. Conclusion: We found significant differences in gut microbiome composition between IBD patients and controls. Specifically, we found a higher abundance of Akkermansia in IBD patients. IBD patients also had a higher alpha diversity than controls. These findings have implications for the prevention and treatment of IBD.

endothelial injury has emerged as a crucial early event in the pathogenesis of microcirculatory dysfunction , capillary leakage and multiorgan dysfunction syndrome . the endothelial - specific angiopoietin ( ang)/tie2 ligand - receptor system has been identified recently as a nonredundant regulator of endothelial responsiveness . ang-1 is a tie2 agonist and promotes endothelial stabilization and quiescence , whereas ang-2 is a tie2 antagonist and promotes endothelial activation , destabilization , and inflammation . while the mediator function of both ang-1 and ang-2 has been well established in preclinical research , only ang-2 has been identified as a clinically useful biomarker in the critical care arena . in the previous issue of critical care , mankhambo and colleagues report on angiogenic factors in malawian children with severe bacterial infection . among those children , diminished levels of the vessel - protective factor ang-1 remained a significant predictor of outcome after multivariate adjustment . whether low ang-1 represents an important risk factor of adverse outcome in critically ill adults remains to be seen .

Colorectal cancer (CRC) remains a major global health challenge. Despite recent advances in treatment, many patients with advanced CRC still suffer from poor outcomes. The most common genetic alteration in CRC is the activation of the KRAS oncogene. The G12C mutation is the most prevalent of all KRAS mutations and represents 50% of all KRAS mutations. KRAS G12C activates the MAPK pathway to stimulate cancer cell growth, proliferation, and survival. In this study, we have developed a novel, highly specific and potent inhibitor of the KRAS G12C mutation. Our preclinical studies showed that this inhibitor could selectively block the activity of KRAS G12C, inhibit cancer cell growth, and sensitize cancer cells to other anti-cancer drugs. We have also demonstrated the efficacy of this compound in vivo using a patient-derived xenograft (PDX) model. Our results suggest that this KRAS G12C inhibitor could be a promising new therapy for patients with advanced CRC.

it is universally recognized that cryopreservation impairs sperm quality . in order to improve postthawing sperm survival and motility , media of different composition and different protocols have been proposed . however , no clear evidence is available to understand which are the most efficient protocol and medium for sperm cryopreservation . the present study evaluates the efficiency of two different cryopreservation protocols and two common freezing media ( fm ) containing different cryoprotectants ( cps ) , test yolk buffer ( tyb ) and sperm freeze ( sf ) , to preserve human sperm quality . our data suggest that tyb is better than sf both in terms of postthaw viability and in terms of progressive motility , while the direct addition of fm to the sperm sample resulted in the most efficient protocol in terms of postthaw viability but not in terms of progressive motility .

Tumor heterogeneity is a major challenge in the treatment of glioblastoma (GBM), the most common and lethal primary brain tumor. Single-cell RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) is a powerful tool that can be used to explore this heterogeneity. In this study, we used RNA-seq to analyze the transcriptomes of individual cells from GBM tumors. We found significant differences in the expression of genes involved in cell cycle control and DNA damage response, suggesting that these pathways may play a role in GBM heterogeneity. We also identified a set of genes that were differentially expressed between GBM cells and normal brain cells, including several oncogenes. Our results suggest that RNA-seq can be used to better understand the complexity of GBM and develop more effective therapies.

Title: Deep Brain Stimulation and the Treatment of Parkinson Disease Symptoms: A Review and New Findings. Background: Parkinson disease (PD) is a progressive neurological condition affecting over 1 million people in the United States. The most common treatment is dopamine replacement therapy, but these therapies become less effective over time and have various side effects. A newer treatment option is deep brain stimulation (DBS), but the benefits of DBS are not fully understood. Methods: This review examines the latest research on DBS for the treatment of PD, including the benefits and risks of the procedure. Results: The review found that DBS is an effective treatment for patients with PD who do not respond to other treatments. DBS can improve motor symptoms such as tremor, rigidity, and slowness of movement. It can also improve cognitive function and quality of life. However, there are risks associated with DBS, including infection and bleeding in the brain. Conclusion: DBS is a promising treatment for patients with PD, but more research is needed to fully understand its benefits and risks. Further research may lead to even more effective treatments for PD.

Background: Glioblastoma (GBM) is the most aggressive and prevalent primary brain tumor with poor overall survival (OS) rates. The current standard of care for GBM is maximal safe surgical resection followed by adjuvant chemoradiation. Despite this, most patients have tumor recurrence within two years due to infiltration of tumor cells into peritumoral areas. There is a need to understand the molecular basis of tumor heterogeneity in GBM.

The human gut microbiome has become increasingly understood in recent years for its crucial role in maintaining human health. Recent studies have highlighted the importance of gut microbiome composition in the development of certain diseases, including inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). We sought to investigate the role of gut microbiome composition in the development of IBD through the use of 16S rRNA sequencing. The study population consisted of 45 patients with IBD and 45 healthy controls. Gut microbiome composition was determined through sequencing of the V3 and V4 regions of the 16S rRNA gene. We observed significant differences in alpha and beta diversity between patients with IBD and healthy controls. At the phylum level, we observed a significant decrease in Bacteroidetes and Actinobacteria in patients with IBD compared to healthy controls. Additionally, at the genus level, we observed significant decreases in Faecalibacterium, Bifidobacterium, and Roseburia in patients with IBD. In recent years, much attention has been given to triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), which accounts for 15% of all breast cancers. TNBC is characterized by an aggressive phenotype and is difficult to treat due to the absence of therapeutic targets. BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations are known to increase the risk of breast and ovarian cancer. However, their roles in TNBC remain unclear. In this study, we analyzed the correlation between BRCA1/2 mutations and TNBC using data from the Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) database. Our results showed a significant association between BRCA1/2 mutations and TNBC (p < 0.001). Moreover, we found that the incidence of BRCA1/2 mutations was higher in TNBC than in other types of breast cancer. These findings suggest that BRCA1/2 mutations play a significant role in TNBC and could be a potential therapeutic target. Our study highlights the importance of further research into the role of BRCA1/2 mutations in TNBC.

In this study, we investigated the relationship between statin use and cardiovascular (CV) risk reduction in patients with no prior history of CV disease (CVD). A systematic review and meta-analysis were performed to compare CV risk between statin users and non-users in a primary prevention setting. Data from 17 randomized controlled trials (RCTs) were included, involving 226,624 patients. Statin use was associated with a 12% reduction in major CV events (relative risk [RR] 0.88, 95% confidence interval [CI] 0.83-0.94, p < 0.001). The number of cardiovascular events prevented per 100 person-years of follow-up was 6.4 in patients receiving statins, compared to 10.5 events in non-users. Title: Targeting tau protein aggregation in frontotemporal dementia: A review of current approaches and future directions. Background: Frontotemporal dementia (FTD) is a common type of dementia characterized by abnormal tau protein aggregation. Methods: We reviewed the literature on tau protein aggregation in FTD. Results: Abnormal tau protein aggregation can lead to microtubule instability, neurofibrillary degeneration, and axonal transport disruption. Additionally, tau aggregation can trigger neuroinflammation, glutamate excitotoxicity, and amyloid deposition. Therapeutic approaches to target tau protein aggregation in FTD include anti-tau antibodies, antisense oligonucleotides, and aggregation modulators. Conclusion: Targeting tau protein aggregation may be a promising approach for treating FTD. Further research is needed to develop effective therapies targeting tau protein aggregation.

Sickle cell disease (SCD) is a genetic blood disorder that causes red blood cells to adopt an abnormal sickled shape. This abnormal shape increases the rigidity of red blood cells, reduces their ability to transport oxygen throughout the body, and causes repeated painful vaso-occlusive crises. In this study, we developed a novel therapeutic approach for treating SCD using CRISPR-Cas9 technology. By targeting the HBB gene encoding β-globin, we were able to achieve efficient correction of the β-globin gene in patient-derived induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs), as well as in human fetal hemoglobin (HbF) inducible erythroid cells. Our approach involves a novel delivery system using lipid nanoparticles that allows for efficient delivery of the CRISPR-Cas9 components into the target cells. We were able to demonstrate that this approach is highly specific and does not cause off-target effects. Our results indicate that this approach is a promising therapeutic option for treating SCD.

the main aim of the study was to assess primary health centers ( phcs ) in terms of availability of assured services , facility of primary management of selected cases , surgeries , maternal and newborn health care services , and child health care services with respect to indian public health standards ( iphs ) . data were collected from service providers ( medical officerin - charge ) at phcs through well - structured questionnaire developed by referring the iphs for phcs prescribed by the directorate general of health services , ministry of health and family welfare , government of india . the study was conducted at five districts ( i.e. bundi , sawaimadhopur , kota , tonk , and karauli ) of rajasthan state of india . all 148 phcs of these five districts were included in the study . findings depict that more than 90% of the study phcs showed availability of services such as outpatient department ( opd ) , antenatal check up ( anc ) , postnatal check up ( pnc ) , management of reproductive tract infections / sexual transmitted infection ( rti / sti ) , immunization , and treatment of diarrhea . however , services such as emergency services ( 24 h ) , primary management of fractures , surgery of cataract , medical termination of pregnancy ( mtp ) services , management of low - birth - weight babies , facility for tubectomy and vasectomy , and facility for internal examination for gynecological conditions were poor at phcs of the study districts , which need to be addressed for further strengthening of primary health centers .

Title: Anti-vascular endothelial growth factor therapy for the treatment of diabetic retinopathy: a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials. Background: Diabetic retinopathy is the leading cause of blindness in working-age adults. The pathogenesis of diabetic retinopathy involves several factors, such as hyperglycemia, oxidative stress, inflammation, and angiogenesis. One of the major angiogenic factors involved in diabetic retinopathy is vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF). Inhibition of VEGF has been shown to be effective in treating diabetic retinopathy in several studies. However, the effectiveness of anti-VEGF therapy in diabetic retinopathy patients has not been systematically evaluated.

Background: Glioblastoma (GBM) is a highly infiltrative and aggressive brain tumor that is difficult to treat. Tumor heterogeneity, or the coexistence of subpopulations of cells with distinct phenotypes within a tumor, contributes to therapeutic resistance and tumor recurrence. Recent advances in sequencing technology have enabled the characterization of tumor heterogeneity at a genomic level. However, the impact of tumor heterogeneity on GBM biology and its potential as a therapeutic target are not well understood.

For patients with Parkinson disease (PD), levodopa is the first‐line treatment for managing motor symptoms. Unfortunately, long‐term use of levodopa can cause motor fluctuations and dyskinesia. Deep brain stimulation (DBS) is a second‐line therapy and has shown great potential in improving PD motor symptoms and reducing levodopa‐induced side effects. However, DBS may lead to surgical complications, which can affect the quality of life of PD patients. In addition, the long‐term efficacy of DBS is still unknown, and the optimal stimulation parameters remain debated.

purposethe purpose of this study was to test a new problem - based learning ( pbl ) method to see if it reinvigorated the learning experience.methoda new pbl format called pbl 2.0 , which met for 90 min two times per week , was introduced in 2009 into an 11-week integrated neuroscience course . one hundred second - year medical students , divided into 10 groups of 10 , who had completed their first year of medical school using a traditional pbl format , participated in pbl 2.0 . students were prohibited from using computers during the first session . learning objectives were distributed at the end of the first day to the small groups , and students were assigned to pairs / trios responsible for leading an interactive discussion on specific learning objectives the following day . student - led lectures were prohibited . all students were responsible for learning all of the learning objectives so that they could participate in their discussions.resultsone hundred and six students were surveyed and 98 submitted answers ( 92% response ) . the majority of groups adhered to the new pbl method . students invested more time preparing the learning objectives . students indicated that the level of interaction among students increased . the majority of students preferred the new pbl format.conclusionspbl 2.0 was effective in increasing student interaction and promoting increased learning .

Objective: To identify non-invasive biomarkers for early-stage Alzheimer disease (AD). Background: Alzheimer disease is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that affects millions of people worldwide. Unfortunately, current diagnostic methods are expensive and do not detect the disease in its early stages. The goal of this study was to identify non-invasive biomarkers that can be used for early-stage AD detection. Methods: We collected samples from patients with early-stage AD and healthy controls. We then used advanced genomic techniques to identify potential biomarkers. Results: Our analysis identified a set of biomarkers that are significantly correlated with early-stage AD. These biomarkers include gene expression signatures, changes in epigenetic markers, and microRNA profiles. Conclusion: Our findings demonstrate the potential of non-invasive biomarkers for early-stage AD detection. These biomarkers may be used to develop a simple blood test that can detect Alzheimer disease in its early stages.

Title: EGFR-Targeted Therapy as Effective as Standard Chemotherapy in Treatment of Non-small Cell Lung Cancer. Background: Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) is a common and deadly type of cancer. Current standard treatments include chemotherapy and radiation, but are often ineffective. New therapies are needed to treat patients with NSCLC. Methods: We conducted a clinical trial of EGFR-targeted therapy (EGFR-TT) versus standard chemotherapy (SC) for patients with NSCLC. Results: We found no significant difference in progression-free survival between the EGFR-TT and SC groups. Conclusion: Our results suggest that EGFR-TT is as effective as SC for the treatment of NSCLC.

Statins, a class of lipid-lowering drugs commonly prescribed to reduce cardiovascular risk, have been the focus of considerable debate in recent years. Although there is evidence that statins effectively reduce cardiovascular risk, some studies have questioned their safety, particularly in patients without a history of cardiovascular disease. In this study, we aimed to examine the association between statin use and cardiovascular risk in a cohort of primary prevention patients.

Background: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a chronic inflammatory disease that affects multiple joints. With the increase in life expectancy, the number of RA patients in the elderly is increasing. Current therapies have shown some benefit in improving symptoms and disease progression. However, there is still a need to develop more effective therapies.

Title: Deep brain stimulation in Parkinson’s disease: a randomized, sham-controlled study of safety and efficacy. Abstract: The number of patients with Parkinson’s disease (PD) is rapidly growing in the United States, imposing a significant burden on the healthcare system. Deep brain stimulation (DBS) is a surgical intervention that can reduce motor symptoms in PD. The safety and efficacy of DBS for the treatment of PD have not been established, as no randomized controlled trials exist. We conducted a double-blind, sham-controlled trial in patients with PD. Participants received either real or sham DBS for six months. DBS was associated with significant improvements in motor function compared to sham, as measured by the Unified Parkinson’s Disease Rating Scale (UPDRS) and Hoehn and Yahr stage. Serious adverse events were more frequent in the sham group. Our results demonstrate that DBS is a safe and effective treatment for patients with PD. sympathetic dysfunction is one of the possible complications of anterior spine surgery ; however , it has been underestimated as a cause of complications . we report two successful experiences of treating severe dysesthetic pain occurring after anterior spine surgery , by performing a sympathetic block . the first patient experienced a burning and stabbing pain in the contralateral upper extremity of approach side used in anterior cervical discectomy and fusion , and underwent a stellate ganglion block with a significant relief of his pain . the second patient complained of a cold sensation and severe unexpected pain in the lower extremity of the contralateral side after anterior lumbar interbody fusion and was treated with lumbar sympathetic block . we aimed to describe sympathetically maintained pain as one of the important causes of early postoperative pain and the treatment option chosen for these cases in detail .

Introduction: Colorectal cancer is one of the most common types of cancer. Despite significant progress in cancer treatments, including chemotherapy, radiation, and surgery, the prognosis for colorectal cancer patients remains poor. KRAS G12C mutations have been identified as an important driver of colorectal cancer. The development of small-molecule inhibitors that specifically target KRAS G12C has led to promising therapeutic options for colorectal cancer patients.

Objectives: The management of melanoma has evolved rapidly in recent years. The discovery of targeted therapies for the treatment of patients with BRAF‐mutant melanoma, and immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) has led to a significant increase in patient survival (Robert et al., 2015; Sosman et al., 2014). However, only a subset of patients treated with immunotherapies derives clinical benefit, and many patients develop resistance to therapy.

The prevalence of type 2 diabetes (T2D) and its associated complications have become an international public health concern. Inhibition of hepatic gluconeogenesis (HGN) is considered an effective strategy in the management of hyperglycemia in T2D. Metformin is a first-line oral drug used in T2D treatment with multiple mechanisms of action. However, the effectiveness of monotherapy is limited and metformin has been used as an adjunct drug in combination therapies. The aim of this study was to examine the potential of metformin as an adjunct therapy in the treatment of T2D by inhibiting HGN. We used a rat model of T2D induced by a high-fat diet and low dose streptozotocin. After treatment with metformin for 12 weeks, the T2D rats showed a significant reduction in fasting blood glucose levels, body weight, and food intake compared to the control group. We found that metformin inhibited HGN enzyme activity by 35% in T2D rats. Additionally, metformin downregulated the expression of HGN genes in the liver

Title: Efficacy and Safety of Bevacizumab for the Treatment of Diabetic Retinopathy: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Clinical Trials. Background: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is the leading cause of blindness in the working-age population worldwide, and its prevalence is rapidly increasing with the rising prevalence of diabetes. Anti-vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) drugs have shown significant efficacy in treating DR in recent randomized clinical trials. However, the efficacy and safety of these drugs for treating DR have not been systematically evaluated.

aims : to evaluate the efficacy of aloe vera mouth rinse on experimental plaque accumulation and gingivitis.materials and methods : in this randomized , controlled , and double - blind study , a total of 148 systemically healthy subjects were screened in the age group of 18 - 25 years . finally , 120 subjects were requested to abstain from oral hygiene ( tooth brushing ) for 14 days and used a specially fabricated plaque guard . following cessation of tooth brushing in the specified area , the subjects were randomly divided into group a ( test group ) who received 100% aloe vera , group b ( negative control group ) who received placebo ( distilled water ) , and group c ( positive control group ) who received 0.2% chlorhexidine . the rinse regimen began on the 15th day and continued for 7 days . plaque accumulation was assessed by plaque index ( pi ) and gingivitis was assessed by modified gingival index ( mgi ) and bleeding index ( bi ) at baseline ( 0 ) , 7th , 14th , and 22nd days.results:there was statistically significant decrease in pi , mgi , and bi scores after the rinse regimen began in both group a ( test group ) and group c ( chlorhexidine ) compared with group b. mouth wash containing aloe vera showed significant reduction of plaque and gingivitis but when compared with chlorhexidine the effect was less significant.conclusion:aloe vera mouthwash can be an effective antiplaque agent and with appropriate refinements in taste and shelf life can be an affordable herbal substitute for chlorhexidine .

Background: The Pfizer-BioNTech (BNT162b2) mRNA vaccine has been shown to be highly effective in preventing COVID-19. However, its immunogenicity has yet to be fully characterized. Our study aimed to investigate the antibody and T-cell immune responses elicited by this vaccine.

Title: RNA-seq analysis of tumor heterogeneity in glioblastoma reveals a previously unrecognized subtype with poor prognosis. Background: Glioblastoma is a highly heterogeneous brain tumor that displays extensive intra-tumoral genetic and epigenetic diversity. While extensive efforts have been made to characterize heterogeneity in glioblastoma at the bulk tissue level, the intra-tumoral cellular diversity has remained largely unexplored.

Background: Triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) is a clinically aggressive and highly heterogeneous type of breast cancer characterized by lack of expression of estrogen receptor (ER), progesterone receptor (PR), and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (HER2). TNBC patients often experience worse prognosis and shorter survival than patients with other types of breast cancer. Accumulating evidence has indicated that germline breast cancer gene (BRCA) mutations are associated with TNBC.

nuclear lamins a / c control several critical cellular functions , e.g. , chromatin organization , gene transcription , dna replication , dna damage responses , cell cycle progression , cell differentiation , and cell polarization during migration . however , few studies have addressed the role of lamins a / c in the control of the functions of immune cells . recently , we have demonstrated that lamins a / c are induced in t cells upon antigen recognition . lamins a / c enhance t cell responses by coupling the plasma membrane to the nucleus via the linker of nucleoskeleton and cytoskeleton ( linc ) complex and the actin cytoskeleton . here , we discuss the possible physiological relevance and functional context of lamin a / c in t cell activation and propose a model in which lamins a / c are key modulators of immune cell functions .

Renal biomarkers hold promise as early indicators of chronic kidney disease (CKD) progression to end-stage renal disease. In this study, we explored the potential of urinary α-1-microglobulin (Uα1MG), N-acetyl-β-2-glycoprotein (UNAG), and β2-microglobulin (Uβ2MG) to identify CKD progression. We measured these urinary biomarkers in a longitudinal cohort of 1,000 patients over 10 years. Uα1MG, UNAG, and Uβ2MG were measured using commercially available enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) kits. CKD progression was defined as a ≥ 30% reduction in eGFR or the initiation of dialysis. Spearman correlations were performed to evaluate biomarker associations with eGFR.
